# Pathway-Specific Exit Strategies

**Generated by Opus 4.6 with Extended Thinking**

This document defines exit strategies for each investment pathway. Different pathways require different time horizons, profit targets, and risk management approaches.

---

## LONG PATHWAYS

### 1. deepValue - Undervalued Quality Compounder
**Thesis:** Quality business trading significantly below intrinsic value due to market inefficiency, not fundamental issues.

**Time Horizon:** 2-5 years

**Initial Take-Profit:** None - hold for thesis

**Stop-Loss:** -15% hard stop OR fundamental deterioration (ROE drops >30%, debt/equity increases >50%, FCF turns negative for 2 consecutive quarters)

**Trailing Stop:** Activate at +100%, trail at -25% from peak

**Profit-Taking Strategy:**
- Trim 25% at +100% (lock in 1x gain)
- Trim 25% at +200% (lock in 2x gain)
- Let remaining 50% run with trailing stop
- Full exit if valuation reaches expensive (P/E >30 or P/B >5, depending on sector)

**Re-evaluation:** Quarterly (earnings reports)

**Exit Conditions:**
- Valuation reaches fair/expensive (P/E exceeds sector median by >50%)
- ROE declines >30% for 2 consecutive quarters
- Debt/equity ratio increases >50%
- Management change with questionable track record
- Thesis plays out (value recognized by market)

**Example:** MSFT bought at $280 (P/E 25) believing intrinsic value is $560. Hold through volatility. Trim at $560 (1x), $840 (2x), trail remainder.

---

### 2. highGrowth - Revenue/Earnings Acceleration
**Thesis:** Company showing accelerating growth metrics with expanding margins and market opportunity.

**Time Horizon:** 6-18 months

**Initial Take-Profit:** +50% (growth stocks can run, but momentum reverses fast)

**Stop-Loss:** -12% hard stop (growth stocks are volatile)

**Trailing Stop:** 
- Activate at +25%
- Trail at -15% from peak (wider trail for volatility)
- Tighten to -10% after +100% gain

**Profit-Taking Strategy:**
- Trim 33% at +50% (lock in gains)
- Trim 33% at +100%
- Trail remaining 34% aggressively
- Never hold full position past +150% without trimming

**Re-evaluation:** Monthly (growth metrics can change quickly)

**Exit Conditions:**
- Revenue growth decelerates 2 consecutive quarters
- Gross margins compress >300bps
- Guidance miss or reduction
- Competitive threat emerges
- Valuation becomes extreme (P/S >20 for SaaS, P/E >60 for others)

**Example:** NVDA at $400 during AI boom. Trim at $600, $800, trail remainder. Exit if data center revenue growth slows.

---

### 3. inflection - Early Turnaround Signal
**Thesis:** Company showing first signs of turnaround - improving metrics after period of decline.

**Time Horizon:** 3-9 months (prove the inflection is real)

**Initial Take-Profit:** +30% (inflections can fail or succeed quickly)

**Stop-Loss:** -10% hard stop (these are speculative)

**Trailing Stop:**
- Activate at +20%
- Trail at -12% from peak

**Profit-Taking Strategy:**
- Trim 50% at +30% (lock in the inflection trade)
- Hold remaining 50% to see if it becomes a full turnaround
- If fundamentals continue improving for 2+ quarters, reclassify to 'turnaround' pathway with longer hold

**Re-evaluation:** Monthly

**Exit Conditions:**
- Inflection fails (metrics reverse)
- 2 quarters of data confirm or deny the inflection
- Management commentary suggests one-time improvement
- Competitive position hasn't actually improved

**Example:** Retailer showing first quarter of positive same-store sales after 8 quarters of decline. Exit if next quarter disappoints.

---

### 4. cashMachine - High FCF Generator
**Thesis:** Stable business generating consistent free cash flow, returning capital via dividends/buybacks.

**Time Horizon:** 2-4 years (income + modest appreciation)

**Initial Take-Profit:** None - hold for cash generation

**Stop-Loss:** -12% hard stop OR dividend cut >25%

**Trailing Stop:**
- Activate at +40%
- Trail at -20% from peak (these are stable, allow volatility)

**Profit-Taking Strategy:**
- Trim 25% at +50% (rebalance)
- Hold core position for income
- Full exit if FCF yield drops below 5% or dividend coverage falls below 1.5x

**Re-evaluation:** Quarterly

**Exit Conditions:**
- FCF declines >20% for 2 consecutive quarters
- Dividend cut or suspension
- Payout ratio exceeds 100% (unsustainable)
- Debt/EBITDA rises above 4x
- Better cash machine opportunity emerges (yield >200bps higher)

**Example:** AT&T at $18 with 7% yield. Hold for income. Exit if dividend cut or FCF deteriorates.

---

### 5. qarp - Quality at Reasonable Price
**Thesis:** High-quality business (high ROE, low debt, consistent growth) trading at reasonable valuation.

**Time Horizon:** 1-3 years

**Initial Take-Profit:** +40% (quality deserves premium, but not infinite)

**Stop-Loss:** -10% hard stop OR quality metrics deteriorate

**Trailing Stop:**
- Activate at +30%
- Trail at -15% from peak

**Profit-Taking Strategy:**
- Trim 33% at +40%
- Trim 33% at +80%
- Trail remaining 34%
- Full exit if valuation becomes expensive (P/E >35 or sector median +100%)

**Re-evaluation:** Quarterly

**Exit Conditions:**
- Valuation no longer reasonable (P/E exceeds sector by >75%)
- Quality deteriorates (ROE drops >25%, debt increases significantly)
- Growth stalls (revenue growth <5% for 2 quarters)
- Better QARP opportunity emerges

**Example:** MSFT at P/E 28 (sector median 25). Quality business, reasonable price. Hold until P/E 40+ or fundamentals weaken.

---

### 6. turnaround - Multi-Year Transformation
**Thesis:** Company undergoing fundamental business transformation (new management, strategy shift, restructuring). Requires patience through volatility.

**Time Horizon:** 2-4 years

**Initial Take-Profit:** None - hold for transformation

**Stop-Loss:** -20% hard stop (need room for volatility) OR transformation clearly failing

**Trailing Stop:**
- Activate at +100%
- Trail at -30% from peak (transformations are volatile)

**Profit-Taking Strategy:**
- Trim 20% at +100% (lock in some gains)
- Trim 30% at +200%
- Hold remaining 50% for full transformation
- Full exit when transformation complete (margins normalize, growth stabilizes)

**Re-evaluation:** Quarterly (track transformation milestones)

**Exit Conditions:**
- Transformation plan abandoned or significantly altered
- Key executives leave
- Debt becomes unsustainable (interest coverage <2x)
- Competitive position worsens despite transformation efforts
- Transformation completes successfully (exit at success)

**Example:** Intel's foundry transformation. Hold through volatility. Exit if foundry plan fails OR succeeds and margins normalize.

---

### 7. value_dip - Temporary Weakness in Quality
**Thesis:** High-quality business experiencing temporary weakness (earnings miss, sector rotation, macro fear). Expecting mean reversion to fair value.

**Time Horizon:** 3-12 months

**Initial Take-Profit:** +20% (exit at fair value recovery)

**Stop-Loss:** -8% hard stop (if dip becomes trend)

**Trailing Stop:**
- Activate at +15%
- Trail at -8% from peak (tight - this is a trade, not investment)

**Profit-Taking Strategy:**
- Trim 50% at +15% (lock in the dip recovery)
- Trim remaining 50% at +25%
- Full exit by +30% - thesis is "temporary dip," not "long-term hold"
- If stock continues higher, that's fine - thesis was dip recovery, not multi-year hold

**Re-evaluation:** Weekly (dips resolve quickly)

**Exit Conditions:**
- Fair value reached (P/E returns to historical median)
- Dip becomes trend (fundamentals actually deteriorating)
- 6 months pass without recovery (dip may be structural)
- Better value_dip opportunity emerges

**Example:** MSFT drops from $420 to $350 on sector rotation (P/E 28→23). Buy the dip. Exit at $420 (fair value restored). If you want long-term MSFT exposure, that's a different thesis (deepValue or qarp).

---

## SHORT PATHWAYS

### 8. overvalued - Valuation Compression
**Thesis:** Quality business trading at unsustainable valuation premium. Expecting multiple compression.

**Time Horizon:** 6-18 months

**Initial Take-Profit:** -25% (cover at reasonable valuation)

**Stop-Loss:** +15% hard stop (if market keeps bidding it up)

**Trailing Stop:**
- Activate at -20% profit
- Trail at +10% from low (cover on bounces)

**Profit-Taking Strategy:**
- Cover 50% at -25%
- Cover remaining 50% at -40% or when valuation normalizes
- Full cover if fundamentals actually improve (thesis broken)

**Re-evaluation:** Monthly

**Exit Conditions:**
- Valuation normalizes (P/E returns to sector median)
- Fundamentals improve beyond expectations (growth accelerates)
- Earnings beat significantly (market may be right)
- Technical breakdown fails to materialize

**Example:** Stock trading at P/E 60 (sector median 25). Short expecting compression to P/E 40. Cover at -25%.

---

### 9. deteriorating - Fundamental Short
**Thesis:** Business fundamentals deteriorating (declining margins, market share loss, debt issues). Expecting continued decline.

**Time Horizon:** 6-12 months

**Initial Take-Profit:** -30%

**Stop-Loss:** +12% hard stop OR fundamentals stabilize

**Trailing Stop:**
- Activate at -20%
- Trail at +8% from low

**Profit-Taking Strategy:**
- Cover 50% at -30%
- Hold remaining 50% if deterioration continues
- Full cover if fundamentals stabilize or improve

**Re-evaluation:** Monthly

**Exit Conditions:**
- Deterioration stops (margins stabilize, market share loss halts)
- Management change with credible turnaround plan
- Activist investor involvement
- Acquisition rumors

**Example:** Retailer losing market share to e-commerce. Short until bankruptcy risk or stabilization.

---

### 10. overextended - Technical Exhaustion
**Thesis:** Stock extended far above moving averages on momentum. Expecting pullback/consolidation.

**Time Horizon:** 2-8 weeks (technical trades are short)

**Initial Take-Profit:** -8% (quick technical pullback)

**Stop-Loss:** +6% hard stop (if momentum continues)

**Trailing Stop:**
- Activate at -5%
- Trail at +3% from low (tight - this is a quick trade)

**Profit-Taking Strategy:**
- Cover 100% at -8% or when technical indicators normalize
- This is a trade, not an investment - take profit quickly

**Re-evaluation:** Daily (technical setups resolve fast)

**Exit Conditions:**
- Pullback occurs (RSI <70, price returns to 20-day MA)
- Momentum continues (new highs on strong volume)
- 4 weeks pass without pullback

**Example:** Stock up 40% in 3 weeks, RSI >80. Short for pullback to 20-day MA. Cover quickly.

---

## IMPLEMENTATION NOTES

### Partial Exit Logic
```javascript
if (gain >= firstTrimLevel) {
  trimPosition(trimPercent);
  updateTrailingStop(tighterTrail);
}
```

### Fundamental Stop Logic
```javascript
if (ROE_decline > 30% && quarters >= 2) {
  exitPosition("fundamental deterioration");
}
```

### Pathway Evolution
```javascript
// value_dip that doesn't recover becomes deteriorating
if (daysHeld > 180 && gain < 0 && fundamentalsWorsening) {
  reclassifyTo("deteriorating");
}

// inflection that succeeds becomes turnaround
if (quartersOfImprovement >= 2 && gain > 30%) {
  reclassifyTo("turnaround");
  updateExitStrategy(longerHorizon);
}
```

### Key Principle
**Exit strategy must match investment thesis.** A "dip buy" is not a "long-term hold" - they're different theses requiring different exits.
